fields.label: Authentication method
fields.description: Choose an authentication method to access the data source

fields.label: User
fields.description: The username of the data source account

fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password of the data source account

fields.label: Add self-signed certificate
fields.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

fields.label: TLS Client Authentication
fields.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

fields.label: ServerName
fields.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

fields.label: Client Certificate
fields.description: The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

fields.label: Client Key
fields.description: The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

ui.placeholder: Begins with --- RSA PRIVATE KEY CERTIFICATE ---

fields.label: Skip TLS certificate validation
fields.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

fields.label: Allowed cookies
fields.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials. Both fields are used together when root.basicAuth is true.
TLS mutual auth: enabling jsonData.tlsAuth requires serverName + client cert + client key. See @grafana/plugin-ui TLSClientAuth.tsx.
Self-signed / custom CA verification: enabling jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert requires the CA certificate PEM in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.

instructions.msg: Parca authentication is built on Grafana's HTTP settings model, not a discriminator field. The editor's three visible auth methods write these flags: 'Basic authentication' sets root.basicAuth=true and provides root.basicAuthUser + secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword; 'Forward OAuth Identity' sets jsonData.oauthPassThru=true; 'No Authentication' leaves both false. Only one of basicAuth / oauthPassThru should be true at a time — the editor's onAuthMethodSelect (@grafana/plugin-ui utils.ts:44-54) always writes both. The upstream utility convertLegacyAuthProps also recognises root.withCredentials for 'cross-site access control', but this plugin does not offer it in its visibleMethods (default [BasicAuth, OAuthForward, NoAuth]).

Minimal Basic-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:7070","basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<user>","jsonData":{},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<password>"}}. Minimal No-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:7070","jsonData":{}}. Minimal OAuth-forward payload: {"url":"http://localhost:7070","jsonData":{"oauthPassThru":true}}. root.url is required — the Parca backend passes settings.URL directly to `NewQueryServiceClient(httpClient, settings.URL, connect.WithGRPCWeb())` (pkg/parca/plugin.go:77) and uses it as the base URL of the Connect/gRPC-web profiling client. No aliasID or legacy plugin ID: the pluginType is `parca` verbatim.

TLS is independent of the auth method. jsonData.tlsAuth=true enables mTLS and requires jsonData.serverName plus secureJsonData.tlsClientCert and secureJsonData.tlsClientKey. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true enables server-cert verification against a custom CA and requires secureJsonData.tlsCACert. Both can be enabled together. jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true bypasses verification entirely and should only be used for testing. All TLS fields are consumed by the SDK's settings.HTTPClientOptions(ctx) call in pkg/parca/plugin.go:65.

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.basicAuthPassword == true) instead of secureJsonData. Custom HTTP header values follow the same pattern via httpHeaderValue<N> secrets, now modeled by the jsonData_httpHeaders indexedPair field (name → jsonData.httpHeaderName<N>, value → secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue<N>, starting at N=1). Parca itself defines no plugin-specific secrets beyond the shared HTTP settings set.

Parca has NO plugin-specific jsonData fields. `src/types.ts:21` declares `ParcaDataSourceOptions extends DataSourceJsonData {}` — an empty interface. Every jsonData field in this schema (tlsAuth, tlsAuthWithCACert, tlsSkipVerify, serverName, timeout, keepCookies, oauthPassThru) is written by @grafana/plugin-ui's Auth/AdvancedHttpSettings components and read by the SDK's HTTPClientOptions, not by any Parca-owned code path. There is no upstream pkg/models/settings.go and no `LoadSettings` to mirror. Custom HTTP headers ARE now modeled by the jsonData_httpHeaders array field using an indexedPair storage mapping: each header's name is written to jsonData.httpHeaderName<N> and its value is written write-only to secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue<N> (starting at N=1), exactly as @grafana/plugin-ui's CustomHeaders component and the SDK HTTPClientOptions expect. The per-header httpHeaderValue<N> secrets are dynamic and are therefore not listed among the static SecureJsonDataKeys.

The Parca datasource plugin is scheduled for deprecation on 2nd of January 2027 (`src/ConfigEditor.tsx:17,27-30`). The config editor renders a `<Alert severity="warning" title="Parca data source is deprecated">` banner above every other field. The deprecation notice is a UI hint and is not persisted in settings; provisioning payloads are unaffected. After the deprecation date, no updates will ship — migrate to a supported profiling backend (e.g. Grafana Pyroscope) before then.
